adjudicator_mark.evidence:
That same digest is then reused for both environments: `deploy-staging` (needs: `build-frontend`, line 22-28) and `deploy-prod` (needs: `[build-frontend, deploy-staging]`, line 29-35) both consume `needs.build-frontend.outputs.digest` — there is no separate `--mode production` build step and no prod-specific artifact.
- **Consequence**: the bundle shipped to production will contain `VITE_API_BASE_URL=https://staging-api.example.com` baked in, since `vite build --mode staging` is the only build that ever runs and it will load `.env.staging` (Vite's mode-based env file convention). The production frontend would call the staging API.

votes.evidence: the bundle shipped to production will contain `VITE_API_BASE_URL=https://staging-api.example.com` baked in, since `vite build --mode staging` is the only build that ever runs ... The production frontend would call the staging API.

disputes.rule: two-of-three majority: the two primary judges' marks plus the adjudicator's mark; because the primaries split one-to-one on a disputed mark, the majority equals the adjudicator's mark, and the mark stays in every denominator
